title: Silent TypeError swallowing masks user callback bugs
description: _llm.py _compile_prompt and _get_llm use cascading try/except TypeError to detect callback signatures. A TypeError inside a user's factory or compiler function (e.g., wrong args to internal call) is silently caught and fallback runs instead. Use inspect.signature at registration time to detect parameter count, not at call time. Source: code review HIGH-02, PP-02, PP-03.

title: configure_llm boilerplate repeated 15+ times in tests
description: configure_llm called inline in 15+ tests. Could be a parameterized fixture. Source: code review LOW-02, DRY-09, TEST-07.
close_reason: configure_fake_llm helper in tests/fakes.py — 23 inline calls down to 4

title: @neograph.tool decorator with signature-inferred schemas
description:
Source: Pydantic AI @agent.tool
Effort: Small
Breaks API: No (keep Tool class alongside)

Problem: Current Tool('search', budget=5) requires a separate register_tool_factory('search', lambda config, tool_config: FakeSearchTool()) call. Two places to update, string coupling, no type safety.

Proposed:
  @neograph.tool(budget=5)
  def search_code(ctx: ToolContext, query: str) -> list[str]:
      '''Search the codebase for the given query.'''
      return ctx.deps.search(query)

  # Use in a Node:
  research = Node('research', mode='gather', tools=[search_code], ...)

Tool name = function name. Args schema = signature. Description = docstring. Dependencies via ToolContext[T].

Implementation:
- @neograph.tool decorator wraps the function with a Tool instance attached
- Auto-registers the factory based on function name
- Introspects signature for argument types
- Tool name defaults to function name (overridable)

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #3
close_reason: @tool decorator shipped — tool name/schema/description auto-inferred from function signature

title: Positional Node("name",...) and Tool("name",...) don't work — breaks all docs
description:
Node and Tool are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses with no custom __init__ (unlike Construct). They only accept keyword args. Every positional-arg usage in the docs raises TypeError: BaseModel.__init__() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given.

Confirmed breaking:
  Node("classify", mode="produce", ...)  # TypeError
  Tool("search_web", budget=3)             # TypeError

Affected files (all break on copy-paste):
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/vocabulary.mdx:45-52, 62-63
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/node-modes.mdx:28, 49, 74, 57-58, 82-83
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/modifiers.mdx:47, 101-108, 148
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/subgraphs.mdx:33-35, 53-54, 58
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/llm-configuration.mdx:71
- website/src/content/docs/getting-started/what-is-neograph.mdx:46-53, 67-68
- website/src/content/docs/getting-started/why-neograph.mdx:135
- website/src/content/docs/getting-started/quick-start.mdx:194
- src/neograph/node.py:4-11 (docstring)
- src/neograph/tool.py:14-15 (docstring)

Fix: add custom __init__ to Node and Tool mirroring Construct's (def __init__(self, name_=None, /, **kwargs)) pattern so positional name works. Single-file change eliminates most doc issues. Source: code review issue #1.

title: Reject underscore-prefixed attributes in _PathRecorder.__getattr__
description:
MED-01 from code review 040426_2207 (PP-03).

_PathRecorder.__getattr__ silently records dunder names. Verified:
  _PathRecorder().__dict__.foo._neo_path  # returns ('__dict__', 'foo')

A user writing 'lambda s: s.__dict__.foo' silently produces Each(over='__dict__.foo', ...) — guaranteed runtime failure with no assembly-time signal.

Fix src/neograph/modifiers.py:37-40:
  def __getattr__(self, name: str) -> '_PathRecorder':
      if name.startswith('_'):
          raise AttributeError(name)
      return _PathRecorder(self._neo_path + (name,))

The existing .map() error handler (after MED-08 narrows it to AttributeError/TypeError) will catch this and emit the 'pure attribute-access chain' message correctly.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-01
close_reason: Wave 2 TDD: red-phase tests written first (dunder reject, underscore reject, user-exception propagates), all failed, then _PathRecorder.__getattr__ gained a leading-underscore rejection and .map()'s except Exception narrowed to (TypeError, AttributeError). 102/102 passing (99 + 3 new).

title: Orphan return None in node.py:91
description:
src/neograph/node.py:91 has an unreachable 'return None' after 'scripted()' classmethod closes:

    # has_modifier, get_modifier, __or__ inherited from Modifiable
        return None

Harmless but ugly and will confuse readers/linters. Fix: delete line 91. Source: code review issue #10.

title: node_id is a required state field but nowhere documented
description:
src/neograph/state.py:99 hardcodes fields["node_id"] = (str, ...). Every run() call must supply input={"node_id": ...} or Pydantic raises ValidationError. Every example does this, but docs never explain why, and Quick Start shows it without comment. A user writing their own pipeline will be blindsided.

Fix: either (a) document node_id as a required initial field with rationale, or (b) make it optional (fields["node_id"] = (str, "")). Preference: drop the hard requirement — it's meaningless to generic users and leaks internal conventions. Source: code review issue #3.

title: has_modifier/get_modifier/__or__ duplicated on Node and Construct
description: node.py and construct.py have identical implementations of has_modifier(), get_modifier(), and __or__(). Extract a ModifierMixin base class or shared mixin. Source: code review HIGH-04, DRY-04.

title: configure_llm doc says RuntimeError, code raises ValueError
description: website/src/content/docs/concepts/llm-configuration.mdx:21 says 'you get a RuntimeError'. Actual: src/neograph/_llm.py:107-108 raises ValueError. Fix: update doc to ValueError. Source: code review issue #8.

title: construct.py + modifiers.py hygiene sweep (Lows)
description:
Grouped Low-severity cleanups from code review 040426_2207. Batch into one PR.

**Structural:**
- LR-02: Extract ~280 LOC of validation helpers (_validate_node_chain, _check_*, _resolve_*, _format_*, _suggest_*, etc.) from construct.py (369 lines → ~90) into src/neograph/_construct_validation.py. construct.py imports _validate_node_chain. No API change.
- LR-03: As a side effect of LR-02, inspect + os + types imports leave the declaration module.

**Error message polish:**
- CON-04: src/neograph/modifiers.py:97-122 — four .map() TypeError messages lack terminal period. Add '.' to each.
- CON-05: src/neograph/construct.py:226 — add a one-line comment explaining why _MISSING is needed ('distinguishes absent field from None-valued annotation'), matching the self-documenting comment style at _llm.py:36.

**Docstring/docs:**
- CON-06: src/neograph/modifiers.py:68-89 — Modifiable.map docstring uses RST :: examples. Restructure with Usage: and Args: headers to match Node.run_isolated at node.py:104-127 (sibling added in the same generation).
- CON-07: tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:331 — TestNodeMap lacks a class-level docstring. Add one-liner matching TestConstructValidation.

**Idiom cleanup:**
- PP-04: src/neograph/modifiers.py:32-35 — replace object.__setattr__(self, '_neo_path', path) with plain 'self._neo_path = path' (cargo cult — no custom __setattr__ exists).
- PP-08: src/neograph/construct.py — drop 'hasattr(types, "UnionType")' guard in _extract_list_element; pyproject.toml requires python >= 3.11 where UnionType is guaranteed.
- PP-13: Optional — src/neograph/construct.py:66,73 — replace 'nodes: list[Any] = []' and 'modifiers: list[Modifier] = []' with Field(default_factory=list) for explicit intent (behavior identical under pydantic v2).
- PP-15: src/neograph/modifiers.py — add '-> Self' return annotation to Modifiable.map and Modifiable.__or__. Import Self from typing.

**Not in scope (informational, no action):**
- LR-04, LR-05, LR-06, LR-07, PP-05, PP-07, PP-10, PP-12, PP-14, PP-16 — flagged but no fix needed.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md Low findings

title: Mutable default dict on Node.llm_config and Tool.config
description: Node(llm_config: dict = {}) and Tool(config: dict = {}) use mutable defaults. Should use Field(default_factory=dict). Pydantic v2 handles this safely but it's bad practice. Source: code review MED-05, PP-01.

title: Error message phrasing inconsistent for must declare
description: state.py:63 says 'must declare output type' while state.py:109 says 'has no output type. Every node must declare output=SomeModel.' Adopt the more helpful longer form. Source: code review LOW-04, CON-10.

title: Unused RunnableConfig as RC import in compiler.py
description: compiler.py:259 imports RunnableConfig as RC but RC is never used. Remove. Source: code review LOW-03, CON-09, PP-13.

title: Callable Node API for fan-in DAGs: node2(node1(...))
description:
Source: Keras Functional API, Pulumi Output[T]
Effort: Medium
Breaks API: No (additive, but this is what Construct should have been)

Current nodes=[a, b, c] is implicitly linear. True DAGs with fan-in require sub-Constructs today. Proposed: Node.__call__ returns a typed NodeHandle[OutputType]; chaining handles builds the graph.

  claims   = decompose()                # NodeHandle[Claims]
  scored   = enrich(claims)             # NodeHandle[ScoredClaims]
  verified = verify(claims, scored)     # fan-in, free
  pipeline = Construct('rw', output=verified)

Wins:
- True DAG with fan-in that current nodes=[] list can't express
- Reads like data flow
- Type-safe: mypy checks claims is Claims, scored is ScoredClaims

Implementation:
- NodeHandle[T] class wrapping (node, output_type)
- Node.__call__ returns NodeHandle
- Construct accepts an output= NodeHandle and walks backward to collect all nodes
- Coexists with current nodes=[...] list mode

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #5

title: Produce mode: support json_mode and text extraction strategies alongside with_structured_output
description: Produce mode always uses llm.with_structured_output(model) which fails on DeepSeek Reasoner/V3 (400: response_format type unavailable). Production pattern: inject JSON schema into prompt + bind response_format=json_object + parse manually. Add llm_config.output_strategy: structured (current), json_mode (schema-in-prompt, widest compat), text (plain text + parse). Default should be json_mode for widest provider support.

title: Typed llm_config via Literal-discriminated overloads (Node.produce, Node.gather)
description:
Source: Rust typestate builder
Effort: Small-medium
Breaks API: Mild (additive, deprecate Node(mode=...) eventually)

Problem: Node(mode='produce', output=Claims, tools=[...]) accepts invalid combinations silently at runtime. mypy has no idea that produce mode doesn't support tools.

Proposed:
  decompose = Node.produce('decompose', output=Claims, model='fast', prompt='x', temperature=0.8)
  verify    = Node.gather('verify', input=Cluster, output=VerifyResult, tools=[search])
  execute   = Node.execute('execute', input=Plan, output=Result, tools=[write_file])
  munge     = Node.scripted('munge', fn='munge_fn', output=Data)

Each factory uses @overload so Node.produce(tools=[...]) is a type error.

Wins:
- Eliminates runtime 'can't use tools in produce mode' surprises
- Autocomplete shows only valid parameters per mode
- mypy/pyright catch misconfigurations statically

Implementation: add Node.produce, Node.gather, Node.execute classmethods with @overload decorators. Keep Node(mode=...) for backward compat with a deprecation warning.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #7

title: Tighten test_map_end_to_end_fanout assertion (and TestEach:319)
description:
HIGH-01 from code review 040426_2207.

The only end-to-end fan-out test uses:
  assert "alpha" in verify_results or len(verify_results) == 2
The 'or' short-circuits — a single-item dict {'alpha': ...} passes even though fan-out should produce two keys. A regression that broke beta dispatch would not be caught.

Verification: python -c "d={'alpha':'x'}; print('alpha' in d or len(d)==2)" → True.

Fix tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:386 and the copy-pasted source at tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:319 (TestEach::test_fanout_over_collection) with:
  assert set(verify_results.keys()) == {'alpha', 'beta'}
  assert verify_results['alpha'].cluster_label == 'alpha'
  assert verify_results['beta'].cluster_label == 'beta'

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md HIGH-01
close_reason: Wave 1: HIGH-01 tightened fan-out assertion in TestEach::test_fanout_over_collection (line 319) and TestNodeMap::test_map_end_to_end_fanout (line 386) with set equality + per-key payload. HIGH-02 renamed construct.py's _type_name to _fmt_type (7 call sites); factory.py's _type_name stays. 99/99 passing.

title: invoke_with_tools mutates messages list in-place
description: _llm.py invoke_with_tools creates messages from _compile_prompt then appends to it throughout the ReAct loop. Should document or copy. Source: code review MED-10, PP-07.

title: TestLLMUnknownToolCall assertion is vacuous
description: test_unknown_tool_call_handled asserts result['explore'] is not None but the value is an AIMessage('ok done'), not a Claims model. Test passes because AIMessage is not None, but production output is wrong. Fix: assert isinstance(result['explore'], Claims) and fix FakeLLMHallucinator to return proper structured output. Source: code review CRIT-03, TEST-01.

title: Quick Start topic passed through input is silently unused
description:
website/src/content/docs/getting-started/quick-start.mdx:120,157 passes 'topic' in run(input=...), but line 157 hardcodes 'microservice authentication' in the prompt compiler. A learner reading this will assume topic is automatically threaded into the prompt — it isn't. They'll later be surprised that changing the topic value changes nothing.

Fix: show the correct pattern — read topic from config["configurable"]["topic"] in the prompt compiler (as examples/vs_langgraph/01_sequential_pipeline.py:99 does). This teaches the config injection pattern explicitly. Source: code review issue #6.

title: modifiers.mdx example missing Tool import
description: website/src/content/docs/concepts/modifiers.mdx:99-109 imports only Node, Each but uses Tool("search_nodes", budget=5). Copy-pasting the snippet raises NameError (in addition to the positional-arg bug). Fix: from neograph import Node, Each, Tool. Source: code review issue #2.

title: Oracle merge error message missing hint suffix
description: compiler.py:226 says 'not registered.' but line 403 says 'not registered. Use register_scripted().' Add the hint. Source: code review MED-02, CON-03.

title: node_start log fields inconsistent across modes
description: factory.py: scripted logs input_type+output_type, produce logs only output_type, gather/execute log tools+budgets but no types. All modes should log input_type and output_type as base fields. Source: code review MED-03, CON-07.

title: Construct.__init__ name_ type mismatch
description: construct.py: name_: str = None — type says str but default is None. Should be name_: str | None = None. Source: code review MED-08, PP-09.

title: State/config type annotations inconsistent across modules
description: factory.py uses state: BaseModel, compiler.py uses state: Any, _llm.py uses config: dict while factory/compiler use RunnableConfig. Standardize on state: Any, config: RunnableConfig. Source: code review MED-06, CON-04, CON-05.

title: Langfuse import path in observability.mdx is stale
description:
website/src/content/docs/concepts/observability.mdx:129 says: from langfuse.callback import CallbackHandler as LangfuseHandler. Raises ModuleNotFoundError on current langfuse (v4+). The working import is: from langfuse.langchain import CallbackHandler (used correctly in examples/observable_pipeline.py:23).

Fix: update the doc to match the working example. Source: code review issue #4.

title: First-class testing: Node.run_isolated() + fake_llm fixture
description:
Source: Prefect .fn, Dagster direct-invocation
Effort: Small
Breaks API: No

Problem: Testing a NeoGraph Node means mocking FakeLLM, registering scripted fns, running full compile()+run() pipeline. Dagster lets you call my_asset(upstream_value) directly; Prefect exposes my_task.fn(*args).

Proposed:
  # Direct invocation for unit tests
  result = decompose.run_isolated(
      input={'topic': 'x'},
      llm=FakeLLM(...),      # optional, uses configured if None
      config={},              # optional
  )

  # Or a pytest fixture for test-scoped config
  @pytest.fixture
  def neograph_fake_llm():
      with neograph.test.fake_llm() as llm:
          yield llm

  def test_decompose(neograph_fake_llm):
      neograph_fake_llm.returns(Claims(items=['a']))
      result = decompose.run_isolated(input={'topic': 'x'})
      assert result.items == ['a']

Implementation: Node.run_isolated(input, llm=None, config=None) calls make_node_fn(self) and invokes it with a minimal state. No graph, no checkpointer. fake_llm() context manager patches configure_llm for the scope.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #9
close_reason: Node.run_isolated() shipped — direct invocation for unit tests without compile/run

title: Inline error messages with source locations (Pydantic v2-style)
description:
Source: Pydantic v2, Prefect, Dagster
Effort: Medium
Breaks API: No

Problem: Current compile errors are technical but not immediately actionable. Best-in-class frameworks compete on errors.

Target:
  neograph.CompileError: Node 'verify' declares input=ClusterGroup but upstream
    'cluster' produces Clusters.
    └─ hint: did you forget .map(lambda s: s.groups) to fan out per cluster?
    at examples/10_full_pipeline.py:225

Implementation:
- Custom exception class with file, line, snippet, hint fields
- inspect.stack() to capture source location at Node/Construct construction
- Catalogue of common failure modes with tailored hints
- Pydantic-style error format with context arrow

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #8

title: No test verifies output type correctness (isinstance)
description: Tests check result['x'] is not None and result['x'].items == [...] but never isinstance(result['x'], ExpectedModel). A node returning the wrong Pydantic type passes all tests. Add isinstance assertions to key tests. Source: code review MED-09, TEST-04.

title: Incomplete Each/replicate rename in compiler
description: compiler.py node-level Each expansion still uses old name: _add_replicate_nodes, replicate_router, variable replicate. Subgraph-level uses _add_each_subgraph, each_router, variable each. Incomplete rename. Rename _add_replicate_nodes to _add_each_nodes, replicate_router to each_router, replicate variable to each. Source: code review HIGH-03, CON-02.

title: TestOperator.test_interrupt_on_failure has bare except
description: Test catches except Exception: pass around the interrupt. Any error (not just GraphInterrupt) is silently swallowed. Resume is commented out. Test proves almost nothing. TestOperatorResume already covers interrupt/resume properly. Delete or fix this test. Source: code review HIGH-05, TEST-02.

title: ToolBudgetTracker.all_exhausted() returns True for empty tool list
description: Edge case: no tools means all_exhausted() is True, which forces immediate response. Source: code review LOW-05, PP-08.

title: vs_langgraph/04_human_in_the_loop.py unused register_scripted import
description: examples/vs_langgraph/04_human_in_the_loop.py:116 imports register_scripted but never uses it (no Node.scripted in the example). Lints as warning, confuses readers. Fix: remove register_scripted from the import. Source: code review issue #11.

title: Adopt msg = ...; raise X(msg) idiom in construct.py raises
description:
MED-06 from code review 040426_2207 (CON-01).

18 pre-existing raise sites across 6 modules (state.py, compiler.py, factory.py, _llm.py, modifiers.py, runner.py) uniformly use the two-line pattern:
  msg = '...'
  raise X(msg)

The four new ConstructError raises diverge, passing the message inline via f-string or helper call:
- src/neograph/construct.py:163
- src/neograph/construct.py:198
- src/neograph/construct.py:208
- src/neograph/construct.py:218

Not enforced by ruff (EM101/EM102 rules off in pyproject.toml), but culturally uniform. Introduce local msg bindings before each raise to match.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-06
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: @node decorator: parameter-name dependency inference (Dagster-style)
description:
Source: Dagster @asset, LlamaIndex @step
Effort: Large
Breaks API: No (additive)

KILLER IDEA #1. The function signature IS the dependency graph. No list, no order, no add_edge.

Current NeoGraph:
  decompose = Node('decompose', mode='produce', output=Claims, ...)
  classify = Node('classify', mode='produce', input=Claims, output=Classified, ...)
  pipeline = Construct('rw', nodes=[decompose, classify])

Dagster-style (proposed):
  @node(mode='produce', output=Claims)
  def decompose(topic: str) -> Claims: ...

  @node(mode='produce', output=Classified)
  def classify(decompose: Claims) -> Classified: ...
  # parameter name matches upstream node name → auto-wires

  pipeline = construct_from_module(my_pipeline_module)

Wins:
- No nodes=[...] list, topology inferred from signatures
- Refactor-safe: rename function → downstream breaks at import time
- Fan-in is free: def report(claims: Claims, scored: Scored, verified: Verified)
- IDE jump-to-definition works on parameter names
- Zero string coupling for topology

Implementation: @node decorator stores metadata on the function. construct_from_module walks a module (or class) of decorated functions, builds the dependency DAG from signatures, emits the same internal IR as today's Node(...) constructor. Both modes coexist.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #1

title: raw_node observability claim is incorrect
description:
Multiple docs claim raw_node gets observability wired around it:
- website/src/content/docs/concepts/vocabulary.mdx:38: 'The framework wires edges, observability, and state around it'
- src/neograph/node.py:107 docstring: same claim

But src/neograph/factory.py:85-86 short-circuits: if node.raw_fn is not None, returns the raw function directly — no observability wrapper, no node_start/node_complete log events. Confirmed by running example 06: no log lines for filter_non_functional.

Fix: either (a) wrap raw_fn in an observability decorator inside make_node_fn (preferred — matches the claim), or (b) update docs to say 'the framework wires edges and state around it — observability is your responsibility in raw_node'. Source: code review issue #5.

title: 8+ FakeLLM classes in tests with identical structure
description: Test file has 8+ FakeLLM class definitions sharing identical structure. Could share a base class or factory. Source: code review LOW-01, DRY-08, TEST-06.
close_reason: Extracted StructuredFake/ReActFake/TextFake to tests/fakes.py — 29 classes down to 3

title: Narrow except Exception in .map() to (TypeError, AttributeError)
description:
MED-08 from code review 040426_2207 (PP-02).

src/neograph/modifiers.py:94-102 wraps all exceptions from source(recorder) as TypeError('pure attribute-access chain'). Legitimate non-access failures (e.g. 'lambda s: 1/0' raises ZeroDivisionError, or a user helper raising ValueError) get mis-reported as 'not a pure attribute chain'.

Fix:
  except (TypeError, AttributeError) as exc:
      ...
Let other exceptions propagate unchanged — they're almost certainly genuine bugs in the user's lambda.

Pairs well with MED-01 (reject underscore-prefixed names in _PathRecorder) — that fix raises AttributeError which this narrowed handler will catch cleanly.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-08
close_reason: Wave 2 TDD: red-phase tests written first (dunder reject, underscore reject, user-exception propagates), all failed, then _PathRecorder.__getattr__ gained a leading-underscore rejection and .map()'s except Exception narrowed to (TypeError, AttributeError). 102/102 passing (99 + 3 new).

title: LLM layer accesses ToolBudgetTracker._budgets private attr
description: _llm.py:248 reads budget_tracker._budgets directly in log message. Add a public property or method. Source: code review LOW-06, LR-08.

title: Structured output parsing duplicated in invoke_structured and invoke_with_tools
description: _llm.py has strategy dispatch (structured/json_mode/text) in both invoke_structured and invoke_with_tools. Extract _parse_output(llm, messages, output_model, strategy). Source: code review MED-07, DRY-06.

title: assemble- vs assemble_ separator inconsistency
description: compiler.py:426 uses assemble-{node.name} (hyphen) while compiler.py:251 uses assemble_{sub.name} (underscore) for the same concept (Each barrier node). Align to underscore. Source: code review MED-01, CON-01.

title: Prefer model_fields[].annotation over typing.get_type_hints in _resolve_field_annotation
description:
MED-10 from code review 040426_2207 (PP-09).

src/neograph/construct.py:229-247 tries typing.get_type_hints(model_class) first; on exception falls back to model_fields[field_name].annotation. The fallback can return a ForwardRef or string annotation, which _extract_list_element silently returns None for, and _types_compatible rejects via 'isinstance(x, type)' check — caller gets silent validation failure instead of clear error.

Pydantic v2 resolves forward refs for any model that successfully imports, so model_fields[name].annotation is typically concrete. The typing.get_type_hints fallback is defensive but masks genuine resolution failures.

Fix:
  def _resolve_field_annotation(model_class: Any, field_name: str) -> Any:
      model_fields = getattr(model_class, 'model_fields', None) or {}
      if field_name not in model_fields:
          return _MISSING
      ann = model_fields[field_name].annotation
      if ann is None or isinstance(ann, (str, typing.ForwardRef)):
          return _MISSING
      return ann

CAVEAT: verify that examples 04 and 10 (which use 'from __future__ import annotations') still work after this change. They currently depend on get_type_hints to resolve ForwardRefs — the new approach depends on pydantic v2 resolving them at model finalization, which should happen for any imported model but needs runtime verification.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-10
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: Compiler builds runtime node functions (layering violation)
description: compiler.py creates ~6 inline closures that call invoke_structured directly for Oracle merge nodes. This is factory.py's responsibility. Compiler should only wire topology, not build runtime node functions. Move all node function creation to factory. Source: code review HIGH-01, LR-01, LR-05, LR-07.

title: gather/execute wrappers are identical code
description: factory.py _make_gather_fn and _make_execute_fn are character-for-character identical. Only docstring differs. Bug fix in one won't reach the other. Extract _make_react_fn(node, mode), delete both wrappers. 5-minute fix. Source: code review CRIT-01, DRY-01.

title: Compile-time type errors at Construct assembly, not at compile() call
description:
Source: Haystack cautionary tale (GitHub issue #8862), Dagster static analysis
Effort: Small-medium
Breaks API: No (catches bugs earlier)

Problem: When a Node declares input=Claims, we check at compile() time whether any upstream produces Claims. Move this check to Construct(nodes=[...]) assembly time via __post_init__ with full Pydantic error messages and source locations from inspect.stack().

Implementation:
- Construct.__post_init__ walks nodes in order
- For each node with declared input, verifies an upstream node produces a compatible output
- Raises a Pydantic-style error with file:line from inspect.stack() frame
- Error includes: node name, declared input, upstream chain, suggestion

Example target:
  neograph.CompileError: Node 'verify' declares input=ClusterGroup but upstream
    'cluster' produces Clusters.
    └─ hint: did you forget .map() to fan out per cluster?
    at examples/10_full_pipeline.py:225

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #4
close_reason: Assembly-time type validation: Construct.__init__ now walks the node chain after super().__init__() to validate each node's declared input against upstream producers. Raises ConstructError (ValueError subclass) with source location + .map() hint when upstream has list[X]. Handles Each path resolution (walks dotted path through model_fields, unwraps list[X], verifies element type). ForwardRefs resolved via get_type_hints for modules using . Defers to runtime when producers empty (first-of-chain) or Each root is runtime-seeded. 14 new tests, 99/99 passing. Examples 04/10 continue to work.

title: prompt_compiler should receive output_model and llm_config for json_mode prompts
description: When output_strategy is json_mode, the prompt compiler needs the output Pydantic model's JSON schema to inject it into the prompt (e.g. 'Return a JSON object matching this schema: ...'). Currently prompt_compiler only receives (template, data, node_name, config). It should also receive output_model and llm_config so consumers can build provider-specific prompts. Without this, json_mode produces FILE blocks instead of JSON because the prompt doesn't ask for JSON.

title: Tighten Construct.input/output annotation from Any to type[BaseModel] | None
description:
MED-11 from code review 040426_2207 (PP-11).

src/neograph/construct.py:69-70 declares:
  input: Any = None   # type[BaseModel] | None
  output: Any = None  # type[BaseModel] | None

The comment states the real type. Pydantic v2 with arbitrary_types_allowed=True accepts type[BaseModel] | None directly (verified: pydantic accepts a BaseModel subclass as a type[BaseModel] value). Leave Node.input as Any because of its dict[str, type] case — that's a legitimate union.

Fix construct.py only:
  input: type[BaseModel] | None = None
  output: type[BaseModel] | None = None

Gives better IDE autocomplete and a more honest field contract.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-11
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: KILLER: Construct.forward() subclass mode for branching logic
description:
Source: DSPy Module.forward, PyTorch nn.Module
Effort: Large (v2 project)
Breaks API: No (new class-based mode)

KILLER IDEA #2. Kills Operator(when='string_key') string dispatch entirely. This is where LangGraph's core pain lives.

Current:
  validate = Node(...) | Operator(when='has_failures')
  # 'has_failures' is a string key into _condition_registry — no type checking

Proposed:
  class Analysis(Construct):
      decompose = Node.produce(output=Claims, ...)
      classify  = Node.produce(output=Classified, ...)
      report    = Node.produce(output=Report, ...)

      def forward(self, topic: str) -> Report:
          claims = self.decompose(topic)
          classified = self.classify(claims)

          # CONTROL FLOW IS JUST PYTHON
          if classified.confidence < 0.7:
              claims = self.decompose(topic, temperature=1.0)
              classified = self.classify(claims)

          if any(c['severity'] == 'critical' for c in classified.issues):
              neograph.interrupt({'reason': 'critical', 'items': classified.issues})

          return self.report(classified)

NeoGraph traces forward() once at compile time (like torch.fx) to build the compiled StateGraph. At runtime, executes like the declarative version.

Wins:
- Conditionals are Python 'if' — no Operator(when='...')
- Loops are Python 'for'
- try/except works
- Type checker sees everything (classified.confidence is a real attribute)
- Testability: instantiate Analysis(), call .forward(topic) directly with fakes

Hard part: tracing. At compile time, self.decompose(topic) doesn't execute — records an edge, returns a placeholder of type Claims. if branches need to be compiled separately with runtime dispatch (same as LangGraph add_conditional_edges, but user-facing it's just Python).

Prior art: torch.fx, jax.make_jaxpr, dspy tracing.

This is an epic — break into subtasks before implementing.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #6

title: _extract_input fails with parameterized generics (dict[str, X]) in isinstance check
description: Node(input=dict[str, ClusterMatchResult]) causes TypeError: isinstance() argument 2 cannot be a parameterized generic. _extract_input does isinstance(val, node.input) which doesn't work with dict[str, X]. Should use get_origin() to check the base type (dict) and optionally validate type args separately.

title: Dedupe _type_name helper across factory.py and construct.py
description:
HIGH-02 from code review 040426_2207. Flagged independently by 2 agents (dry + consistency).

Two definitions:
- src/neograph/factory.py:71 — _type_name(t: Any) -> str | None (returns None for None input)
- src/neograph/construct.py:282 — _type_name(tp: Any) -> str (returns literal 'None' for None input)

Same name, divergent return types and None handling. grep -n '^def _type_name' src/neograph/ returns both.

Lowest-friction fix: rename the construct.py version to _fmt_type. The two helpers serve different roles — factory uses None for log-field omission, construct uses 'None' for error rendering. Trying to merge them requires settling the None convention and risking behavioral changes in logging.

Alternative: promote a single canonical helper to a shared module (e.g. src/neograph/_types.py) with one agreed-upon None convention.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md HIGH-02
close_reason: Wave 1: HIGH-01 tightened fan-out assertion in TestEach::test_fanout_over_collection (line 319) and TestNodeMap::test_map_end_to_end_fanout (line 386) with set equality + per-key payload. HIGH-02 renamed construct.py's _type_name to _fmt_type (7 call sites); factory.py's _type_name stays. 99/99 passing.

title: Each over= examples inconsistent and meaningless in isolation
description:
Different over= paths appear across docs with no shared context:
- clusters.clusters (what-is-neograph)
- clusters.groups (vocabulary, modifiers, subgraphs — 6x)
- items.groups (api.mdx:179)
- discover.groups (each-fanout, api.mdx:241)
- cluster.groups (full-pipeline)
- items (quick-start:195)

Several point at hypothetical state fields that the surrounding snippet never defines. The first part of 'over' is a state field name (a previous node's output), so without a named previous node the example is unrunnable.

Fix: settle on one canonical example (e.g. the each-fanout walkthrough's discover.groups with full context) and reference it by link from vocabulary/modifiers/subgraphs. In docs that don't have a full pipeline, use over=<prev_node>.<field> as a placeholder to signal the pattern. Source: code review issue #7.

title: Tighten TestConstructValidation assertions and add missing Each deferral test
description:
Bundle of MED-03, MED-04, MED-05, TQ-05, TQ-06, DRY-04 from code review 040426_2207.

MED-03 (TQ-02): tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:1762 — test_mismatch_hint_suggests_map only matches 'did you forget to fan out' but doesn't pin the suggested field. A bug that emitted the wrong field (e.g. 's.b.other' instead of 's.a.groups') would pass. Add: assert 's.a.groups' in msg

MED-04 (TQ-03): tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:1753 — test_plain_input_mismatch_raises matches only the header 'declares input=Claims'. Doesn't verify the 'upstream producers:' block. Add: assert "node 'a': RawText" in msg

MED-05 (TQ-04): src/neograph/construct.py:189 — Each root-not-in-producers deferral branch is uncovered by any Construct(...) call. Add positive test:
    def test_top_level_each_deferred_to_runtime(self):
        process = Node.scripted('process', fn='f', input=ClusterGroup, output=MatchResult) | Each(over='seeded_from_runtime.groups', key='label')
        Construct('top-each', nodes=[process])  # no error

TQ-05: tests/test_e2e_piarch_ready.py:1837 — test_sub_construct_input_mismatch_in_parent regex 'sub.*declares input=Claims' too loose. Anchor on both the construct name ('sub-construct "sub"') and the clause ('declares input=Claims').

TQ-06: 6 'did not raise' happy-path tests without post-assembly assertions (test_valid_chain_passes, test_each_correct_path_passes, test_first_item_with_input_deferred_to_runtime, test_sub_construct_input_port_satisfies_inner_node, test_sub_construct_chained_in_parent, test_dict_input_skipped). Add per-test assertions that the resulting construct surface is as expected (node count, modifier shape).

DRY-04: 12 tests in TestConstructValidation repeat Node.scripted('a', fn='f', output=X) / Node.scripted('b', fn='f', input=Y, output=Z). Extract two small factory helpers _producer(name, out) and _consumer(name, in_, out) near the class to strip ~20 lines and make each test's assertion the visible part.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-03, MED-04, MED-05, TQ-05, TQ-06, DRY-04

title: .map() method replacing Each(over=..., key=...) string path
description:
Source: Prefect .map(), Airflow .expand()
Effort: Medium
Breaks API: No (additive, keeps Each internally)

Problem: Each(over='cluster.groups', key='label') is a string-pathed access to state. Fragile, invisible to type checkers, no IDE autocomplete, breaks on refactor.

Proposed:
  # Option A: lambda (mypy-friendly)
  verify = Node('verify', input=ClusterGroup, output=VerifyResult).map(
      lambda s: s.cluster.groups, key='label'
  )

  # Option B: with param inference (best)
  verify = Node('verify', input=ClusterGroup, output=VerifyResult).map(clusters)
  # 'clusters' is a variable bound to an upstream node handle

Internals: .map() is sugar over Each — it still builds the same modifier. Implementation is a method on Node that returns self | Each(over=..., key=...). The lambda is introspected or executed once at compile time to resolve the path.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #2
close_reason: Implemented .map() as sugar over Each on Modifiable. Supports lambda introspection via _PathRecorder and string passthrough. 9 new tests covering lambda resolution, string form, semantic equivalence to | Each, end-to-end fan-out, error cases (empty path, scalar return, indexing, wrong type), and Construct inheritance. 85/85 passing.

title: Signature-detection mechanism documented incorrectly
description:
website/src/content/docs/concepts/llm-configuration.mdx:62,138-143,284-289 and website/src/content/docs/reference/api.mdx:371 describe the fallback as 'tries signatures from most specific to least specific' or 'auto-detected via TypeError fallback'. Actual implementation (src/neograph/_llm.py:39-52, 105-137) inspects the callable once with inspect.signature at configure_llm() time and filters kwargs per call. There is no try/except retry loop.

Fix: rewrite as 'The framework inspects your callable at registration time (inspect.signature), then passes only the kwargs it accepts on each call. Accepts **kwargs functions.' Source: code review issue #9.

title: RuntimeError outlier — should be ValueError
description: _llm.py:83 is the only RuntimeError in the codebase. All other config errors use ValueError. Change to ValueError for consistency. Source: code review MED-04, CON-06.

title: Document ConstructError multi-line error format as deliberate package divergence
description:
MED-07 from code review 040426_2207 (CON-03).

13 existing errors across the package are single-sentence period-terminated 'Subject "name" problem. Remediation.' messages (see state.py:63, compiler.py:92, factory.py:57). New ConstructError messages in construct.py are multi-line with embedded newlines, 'hint:' lines, and 'at file:line' suffixes.

The richer format is defensible — assembly-time type errors benefit from structured help. But the divergence is novel and breaks reader expectations.

Two options:
1. Add a short comment near _format_no_producer_error explaining the structured-help exception, so future additions to state.py/compiler.py can adopt it deliberately.
2. Promote the pattern: refactor state.py/compiler.py errors to also use structured help (hints, source locations). Larger but more consistent.

Recommended: option 1 (low-friction). Consider option 2 as a separate enhancement.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-07
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: Extract split_each_path shared helper for Each.over parsing
description:
MED-02 from code review 040426_2207 (DRY-02 + LR-01). Flagged independently by 2 agents (dry + layering).

Each.over dotted-path parsing is implemented twice:
- src/neograph/construct.py:176 — walks types via model_fields (static check)
- src/neograph/compiler.py:265 — walks values via getattr (runtime router)

Both do each.over.split('.') and treat parts[0] as root, parts[1:] as segments. If Each.over ever grows indexing, wildcards, or escaping, the walkers will drift. The whole point of assembly-time validation is to prevent runtime surprises — diverging parsers defeat that goal.

Fix: extract a small helper (e.g. in modifiers.py near Each, or a new _paths.py):
  def split_each_path(over: str) -> tuple[str, tuple[str, ...]]:
      parts = over.split('.')
      return parts[0], tuple(parts[1:])

Both walkers call it; each keeps its own type-vs-value stepping loop.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-02
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: Switch _source_location from inspect.stack() to sys._getframe
description:
MED-09 from code review 040426_2207 (PP-06).

src/neograph/construct.py:337-368 uses inspect.stack() to walk frames for the user-code source location. ~50x more expensive than necessary because inspect.stack() materializes source context for every frame.

Only invoked on the error path (confirmed), so not a happy-path concern — but on failing test suites that assemble many bad Constructs, overhead compounds.

Fix: rewrite using sys._getframe(1) + frame.f_back walk:
  def _source_location() -> str | None:
      try:
          frame = sys._getframe(1)
          while frame is not None:
              module_name = frame.f_globals.get('__name__', '')
              if not (module_name == 'neograph'
                      or module_name.startswith('neograph.')
                      or module_name.startswith('pydantic')):
                  fname = frame.f_code.co_filename
                  if fname and not fname.startswith('<'):
                      return f'{os.path.basename(fname)}:{frame.f_lineno}'
              frame = frame.f_back
      except Exception:
          return None
      return None

Eliminates the try/finally: del stack dance. Combine with the _construct_validation.py split (hygiene sweep) to also drop the inspect import from a declaration module.

Source: .claude/code-review/040426_2207/synthesis.md MED-09
close_reason: Wave 3 refactors. MED-02 extracted split_each_path helper in modifiers.py; both construct.py static walker and compiler.py runtime walker consume it. MED-06 adopted msg=...; raise idiom for 4 ConstructError sites. MED-09 rewrote _source_location using sys._getframe (dropped inspect import). MED-10 hardened _resolve_field_annotation to return _MISSING for unresolved ForwardRef/str leaks. MED-11 tightened Construct.input/output to type[BaseModel] | None. MED-07 added explanatory comment above _format_no_producer_error documenting the intentional multi-line divergence. 102/102 passing; examples 04/10 continue to run (ForwardRef resolution intact).

title: Labeled step syntax à la Beam: 'name' >> Node.produce(...)
description:
Source: Apache Beam 'Label' >> transform
Effort: Small
Breaks API: No (additive sugar)

Beam's pattern: every step has a human-readable label inline. Cheap consistency win — node names always visible at the left edge, searchable in code.

Proposed:
  pipeline = Construct('rw',
      'decompose' >> Node.produce(output=Claims, model='reason')
    | 'classify'  >> Node.produce(input=Claims, output=Classified)
    | 'verify'    >> Node.gather(input=Cluster, output=VerifyResult).map(...)
  )

Implementation: overload str.__rshift__ via Node classmethod that binds the name. str >> Node(...) calls a factory that sets .name.

Minor win, but reads well. Skip if we do #1 (param-name inference) since that removes the need for explicit names.

Source: .claude/research/dx_research_040426.md #10

title: Oracle/Each topology duplicated for Node vs Construct targets
description: compiler.py has near-identical implementations for Oracle (lines 166-236 vs 336-413) and Each (lines 239-295 vs 416-459) for Construct vs Node targets. ~120 lines duplicated. Already diverging: error message missing hint (line 226), separator inconsistency (assemble_ vs assemble-), naming split (replicate vs each). Extract shared _wire_oracle_topology() and _wire_each_topology() helpers. Source: code review CRIT-02, DRY-02, DRY-03, CON-08.
